AI Embodiment Palette
Palette Extraction

Coastal Beacon

2 source images · 8 palette options · Light & Dark modes

Beacon
Abyssal Calm
Monochromatic · Single hue, varying value & saturation
A pure deep-blue palette that stays within the navy family. The warm beacon white serves as the sole accent — a single point of light in the deep. Ideal for an AI presence that feels contemplative, deep, and quietly intelligent. Maximum elegance, minimal distraction.

Twilight Depths
Analogous · Adjacent hues: Indigo → Blue → Teal
Extends the deep blue into neighboring indigo-violet and teal-cyan. Creates a richer, more dimensional color world while staying cool and harmonious. The spectrum from purple-blue through true blue to green-blue gives the embodiment a sense of depth and complexity — intelligence expressed through color range.

ACC 10%
Signal Fire
Complementary · Opposite hues: Deep Blue ↔ Warm Amber
Takes the lighthouse's warm white beacon and intensifies it into a true amber-gold accent against the deep navy. High contrast, immediate visual tension. The embodiment glows warm against a cool depth — intelligence as a flame in the dark. Use sparingly: the amber is the 10% accent that commands attention. Best for the "speaking" and "active" states.
Light Mode
Primary
#1A3A6E

AMB 10%
Beacon & Ember
Split-Complementary · Blue + Warm Coral + Soft Gold
Splits the amber complement into two warm neighbors — a coral-rose and a soft gold. More nuanced than pure complementary. The coral brings emotional warmth (ideal for the embodiment's "listening" state) while the gold signals active energy ("speaking" state). The deep blue remains the grounding dominant. Most versatile for communicating multiple states through color shifts.

#D0C0E8
Pale Lilac
Prismatic Flow
Triadic · Three equidistant hues: Violet, Cyan, Amber
Directly mirrors the iridescent image — three vivid hues in dynamic tension. Violet anchors as primary, cyan provides cool energy, amber brings warmth. This palette is maximally expressive — the embodiment could shift between all three hues to communicate different states. High energy, high personality. Requires careful application of the 60-30-10 rule to avoid visual overload.

Chromatic Silk
Analogous · Adjacent hues: Violet → Blue → Cyan
Strips away the amber warmth and rides the cool side of the iridescence — violet flowing into blue into cyan. Harmonious and fluid, like light bending through glass. The warm amber becomes a sparse accent that pops against the cool flow. Feels ethereal, technological, and alive. The embodiment would shimmer between these adjacent hues like oil on water.

Liquid Amethyst
Complementary · Opposite hues: Deep Violet ↔ Warm Gold
Strips the iridescence down to its core tension — violet depth vs. golden warmth. The most dramatic palette. The embodiment would live in a violet world with gold as its signal color — the moment it speaks or activates, gold flares. Luxurious, confident, slightly mystical. Works beautifully in dark mode where the violet depth can really breathe.
Light Mode

GLD 10%
Opalescent
Split-Complementary · Violet + Rose + Cyan
Keeps the iridescent shimmer but tames it. Rose and cyan flank the violet — both naturally present in the source image's reflections. The palette feels like light trapped inside a gemstone: cool, luminous, and ever-so-slightly warm. Rose brings approachability (important for a daily-use personal assistant), while cyan keeps it modern and sharp. The embodiment would glow between these like shifting facets.
